name: mclocks-preview
description: Open Markdown, JSON, YAML, TOML, XML, INI, and similar files in mclocks via scripts/mc-preview from Explorer or editor context menus.

explorer/context.when: !explorerResourceIsFolder && (resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown || resourceExtname == .json || resourceExtname == .yaml || resourceExtname == .yml || resourceExtname == .toml || resourceExtname == .xml || resourceExtname == .ini || resourceExtname == .config || resourceExtname == .cfg || resourceLangId == markdown || resourceLangId == json || resourceLangId == jsonc || resourceLangId == yaml || resourceLangId == xml || resourceLangId == toml || resourceLangId == ini)

editor/context.when: resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown || resourceExtname == .json || resourceExtname == .yaml || resourceExtname == .yml || resourceExtname == .toml || resourceExtname == .xml || resourceExtname == .ini || resourceExtname == .config || resourceExtname == .cfg || resourceLangId == markdown || resourceLangId == json || resourceLangId == jsonc || resourceLangId == yaml || resourceLangId == xml || resourceLangId == toml || resourceLangId == ini

editor/title/context.when: resourceExtname == .md || resourceExtname == .markdown || resourceExtname == .json || resourceExtname == .yaml || resourceExtname == .yml || resourceExtname == .toml || resourceExtname == .xml || resourceExtname == .ini || resourceExtname == .config || resourceExtname == .cfg || resourceLangId == markdown || resourceLangId == json || resourceLangId == jsonc || resourceLangId == yaml || resourceLangId == xml || resourceLangId == toml || resourceLangId == ini

mclocks.mcPreview.debug.description: Append verbose traces to Output → mclocks Preview (POST URL, path line, curl exit code and body).

mclocks.mcPreview.transport.markdownDescription:
- **auto**: always `curl` / `curl.exe` (single-line absolute path POST). Matches `curl -X POST …/preview --data-binary <path>`.
- **curl**: Same as auto.
- **bash**: use `scripts/mc-preview` (MCLOCKS_* env, optional cwd line).

mclocks.mcPreview.wslMapMntCToWindowsDri.description: WSL/Linux: if the file path is under /mnt/<drive>/..., POST a Windows drive path (e.g. C:/...) so a Windows-hosted mclocks can open the file. Set false if mclocks runs inside WSL and expects /mnt/ paths.

mclocks.mcPreview.wslMapLocalhostToWindo.description: WSL guest: when webHost is 127.0.0.1 or localhost, use the Windows host IPv4 from /etc/resolv.conf (first nameserver) so POST reaches mclocks on Windows. Set false if mclocks runs inside this WSL distro.

mclocks.mcPreview.wslMapLinuxFsToWindows.description: WSL: for Linux FS paths (e.g. /home/...), run wslpath -w so Windows mclocks receives a UNC path. Disable if mclocks runs inside the same WSL distro.

mclocks.mcPreview.wslInvokeWindowsCurl.description: WSL: before Linux curl, run /mnt/c/Windows/System32/curl.exe against http://127.0.0.1:<port>/preview so the TCP client is Windows (reaches mclocks bound to 127.0.0.1). Disable to only use Linux curl + host IP.

mclocks.mcPreview.wslWindowsCurlPath.description: WSL: full path to Windows curl.exe if not at System32 (e.g. /mnt/c/Windows/System32/curl.exe).

mclocks.mcPreview.bashExecutable.description: Bash interpreter (transport bash only). Empty: bash.exe / bash on PATH.

mclocks.mcPreview.scriptPath.description: Absolute path to mc-preview (transport bash only). Empty: <workspace>/scripts/mc-preview.

mclocks.mcPreview.webHost.description: Logical HTTP host you want (typically 127.0.0.1). Under WSL, localhost may be rewritten — see wslMapLocalhostToWindowsHost. MCLOCKS_WEB_HOST when transport is bash.

mclocks.mcPreview.webPort.description: Main HTTP port used for POST /preview (default 3030). MCLOCKS_WEB_PORT when transport is bash.
